sources: ssrn-274973  — Fernandez 2004: The Value of Tax Shields is NOT Equal to PV of Tax Shields
ssrn-256987  — Fernandez 2007: Valuing Companies by Cash Flow Discounting (10 methods)
ssrn-1620871 — Fernandez 2010: WACC: Definition, Misconceptions and Errors
ssrn-LEVERING — Fernandez 2006: Levered and Unlevered Beta
_meta.governing_rule: Only Group D (DCF) methods are conceptually correct for going-concern valuation. Group A (balance sheet), B (multiples), and C (goodwill) are used only as cross-checks or when DCF inputs are unavailable.
_meta.critical_axiom: All 10 DCF methods must yield the SAME equity value when applied consistently. Divergence indicates an input inconsistency — not a model disagreement. Source: ssrn-256987 eq[1]–[10].
_meta.value_vs_price: Value is buyer-specific and assumes a specific discount rate. Price is determined by the market. They may differ substantially. Source: ssrn-743229 §3.

GROUP_A_BALANCE_SHEET.reason: Does not capture future earnings capacity. Book value reflects past decisions, not future cash flows. Source: ssrn-743229 §2.1–2.6.
GROUP_A_BALANCE_SHEET.use: Only as floor estimate (liquidation scenario) or sanity check on net asset value

GROUP_B_MULTIPLES.reason: Relative valuation assumes peers are correctly valued — a circular assumption. Source: ssrn-743229 §2.7–2.11.
GROUP_B_MULTIPLES.use: Sanity check against DCF intrinsic value; primary model for pre-profit companies where FCF is negative and DCF is unreliable

GROUP_C_GOODWILL.reason: Conceptually inconsistent — mixes accounting constructs with market-based valuation. Source: ssrn-743229 §2.12–2.14.
GROUP_C_GOODWILL.use: Never use as primary or cross-check

GROUP_D_DCF.reason: Conceptually correct. All 10 methods give identical equity value under consistent inputs. Source: ssrn-256987, ssrn-1620871.
GROUP_D_DCF.consistency_requirement: E_t + D_t = (E_{t-1} + D_{t-1})(1 + WACC_t) - FCF_t for every period t. Source: ssrn-1620871 eq[4],[5].

D1_ECF_Ke.label: Equity Cash Flow / Ke
D1_ECF_Ke.note: Ke is the required return to equity (CAPM). E_n is terminal equity value.

D2_FCF_WACC.label: Free Cash Flow / WACC
D2_FCF_WACC.iterative_note: E and D in WACC must be the VALUATION-DERIVED values (not market or book). Requires iteration. Source: ssrn-1620871 §3.

D3_CCF_WACCbt.label: Capital Cash Flow / WACC before tax
D3_CCF_WACCbt.ccf_definition: CCF_t = ECF_t + I_t \cdot T = FCF_t + I_t \cdot T

D4_APV.label: Adjusted Present Value
D4_APV.vts: See GROUP_E VTS theories — theory selection is the primary variable

GROUP_E_VTS_THEORIES.governing_rule: VTS theory selection is the ONLY genuine disagreement in valuation theory. All other differences derive from input inconsistency. Source: ssrn-256987 §4.
GROUP_E_VTS_THEORIES.toro_equity_range: Same company (Toro Inc.), same inputs, different VTS theory → equity range 3,335 (Miller) to 4,081 (MM). Source: ssrn-256987 Table 1.

T1_Fernandez_2007.label: Fernandez (2007) — Recommended Default
T1_Fernandez_2007.risk_assumption: VTS has same risk as the unlevered firm (Ku), not debt (Kd or RF)
T1_Fernandez_2007.proof: VTS = D_t \cdot T when debt is rebalanced each period to maintain D/(E+D). Derived from G^u - G^L = D \cdot T. Source: ssrn-274973 Proposition 1.

T2_Miles_Ezzell.label: Miles-Ezzell
T2_Miles_Ezzell.risk_assumption: First period: Kd risk (debt is known). Subsequent periods: Ku risk.

T3_Modigliani_Miller.label: Modigliani-Miller (1963)
T3_Modigliani_Miller.risk_assumption: VTS has same risk as debt (RF) — only applicable for FIXED, KNOWN debt schedules

T4_Myers.label: Myers (1974)
T4_Myers.risk_assumption: VTS has same risk as debt (Kd) — applicable for preset/known debt schedules

T5_Miller.label: Miller (1977)
T5_Miller.risk_assumption: No benefit from leverage due to personal taxes offsetting corporate tax shields

T6_Harris_Pringle_Ruback.label: Harris-Pringle / Ruback
T6_Harris_Pringle_Ruback.risk_assumption: VTS discounted at Ku — implies VTS does not add to firm risk
T6_Harris_Pringle_Ruback.incompatibility_warning: Implies leverage cost when combined with Fernandez beta levering formula. Source: ssrn-1025424 Error category 4.3.

T7_Damodaran.label: Damodaran (1994)
T7_Damodaran.vts_formula: VTS = PV[K_u; D \cdot T \cdot K_u - D(K_d - RF)(1-T)]
T7_Damodaran.risk_assumption: Includes a leverage cost term D(Kd-RF) which penalizes excess Kd over RF
T7_Damodaran.incompatibility_warning: Damodaran beta formula is inconsistent with Fernandez VTS — using both double-counts leverage costs. Source: ssrn-1025424 Error 4.3.

T8_Practitioners.label: Practitioners
T8_Practitioners.vts_formula: VTS = PV[K_u; T \cdot D \cdot K_d - D(K_d - RF)]
T8_Practitioners.risk_assumption: Treats entire spread Kd-RF as a leverage cost — most conservative estimate of VTS

T9_With_Cost_of_Leverage.label: With Cost of Leverage
T9_With_Cost_of_Leverage.vts_formula: VTS = PV[K_u; D(K_u \cdot T + RF - K_d)]

BETA_LEVERING_FORMULAS.governing_rule: Use Fernandez (2007) as default. The choice of levering formula is tied to VTS theory — mixing formulas from different theories introduces leverage cost errors. Source: ssrn-LEVERING.

fernandez_2007.note: βd ≈ 0 simplification: βL ≈ βu(1 + D(1-T)/E) — less accurate but common

harris_pringle.warning: Ignores tax shield on debt — implies lower leverage than Fernandez

damodaran.warning: Implies leverage cost when used with Fernandez VTS. See incompatibility warning in T7_Damodaran.

WACC_DEFINITION.correct_definition: WACC is the rate at which FCF must be discounted to yield the same equity value as discounting ECF at Ke. It is NOT a cost — it is a definitional rate. Source: ssrn-1620871 §1.
WACC_DEFINITION.critical: E and D are VALUATION-DERIVED (iterative), not book value or market cap. Source: ssrn-1620871 §3.

constant_growth_fernandez.consistency_check: g < WACC required (otherwise PV is infinite or negative)

gordon_growth.warning: FCF_1 must be normalized (steady-state, not inflated by one-time items). Source: ssrn-1025424 Error I.7.4.
gordon_growth.residual_value_weight: 60–80% of total firm value. Errors here are disproportionately damaging. Source: ssrn-1025424 §I.7.

D_vs_N_distinction.N: Book value / face value of debt
D_vs_N_distinction.note: When D ≠ N (distressed debt, high-yield bonds), use N in all debt-related formulas. Most public companies: D ≈ N. Source: ssrn-1620871 §5.
